summary: Test Run modal ('Run Test' — POST /agents/test-run) throws a raw, truncated Zod schema-validation error directly into the UI for 18 of the 22 catalog agents selectable in its own dropdown (all 12 'Planned' agents plus 6 real, currently-Active deterministic agents: Job Discovery, ATS Optimization, Match Scoring, Job Matching, Skill Gap, Orchestration). Only the 4 true LLM-backed agents (Resume Tailoring, Cover Letter, Email, Story Extraction) work correctly.
reproduction: 1. Log in as admin/admin123 (canonical login); confirm GET /api/billing/entitlement -> active_paid:true, plan.id:'pro'.
3. Click the header 'Test Run' button (data-testid=test-run-open) to open the Test Run modal.

4. Inspect the 'Select agent' dropdown (data-testid=test-run-select): it lists all 22 catalog agents (options filtered only by `agent.enabled`, which defaults true for every card including Planned ones — see apps/web/src/components/agents/TestRunModal.tsx:29, `agents.filter((a) => a.enabled)`, with no check on `runnable` or `status !== 'planned'`).
5. Select 'Compliance Agent' (a Planned/coming-soon card with no backend) and click 'Run Test' (data-testid=test-run-go).

6. Observe: the network call POST /agents/test-run returns HTTP 200 with body {"model":null,"estTokens":null,"estCost":null,"actualCost":null,"actualTokens":null,"responseSeconds":null,"creditsCharged":0.0} (a legitimate, documented, honest null-shape per the endpoint's own docstring at apps/api/app/routers/agents.py:2178-2224). The frontend's TestRunSchema (apps/web/src/components/agents/api.ts:89-100) declares model/estTokens/estCost/actualCost/actualTokens/responseSeconds as REQUIRED non-nullable z.string()/z.number() with no `.nullable()`; parsing this valid 200 response throws a ZodError, caught by TestRunModal.tsx:96-97 and rendered verbatim (first 140 chars) as the on-screen error: `[{"code":"invalid_type","expected":"string","received":"null","path":["model"],"message":"Expec` (cut off mid-word by the 140-char slice).

7. Repeat step 5-6 with any of: submission, salaryIntelligence, interviewPrep, companyResearch, recruiterOutreach, marketTrends, scheduling, sentimentAnalysis, reference, learningFeedback, notification (the 11 other Planned cards) -> identical raw-JSON error class.

8. Repeat with jobDiscovery, atsOptimization, matchScoring, jobMatching, skillGap, orchestration (6 real, currently 'Active'-status deterministic-backend agents) -> same failure. Root cause confirmed via curl: GET /agents/catalog applies a `model = _model_for_agent(backend) or "deterministic"` fallback (apps/api/app/routers/agents.py:1501) so these show model:'deterministic' on their cards, but POST /agents/test-run (line 2192: `model = _model_for_agent(backend) if backend else None`) omits that same fallback and returns raw null for the identical backend value, so every deterministic (non-LLM) agent breaks Test Run even though its own card and Run button work perfectly.

9. Control: select 'Story Extraction Agent' (a real LLM-backed agent) and click 'Run Test' -> succeeds normally, renders 'Dry-run complete. Agent responded in 22.4s. Actual cost $0.001 · 523 tokens. No credits were charged for this preview.' -- confirming the modal itself, the honest non-billing contract ('creditsCharged:0.0'), and the 4 working agents are genuinely fine; the defect is isolated to the schema/endpoint contract mismatch for the other 18.
10. Reproduced in a SECOND, independent fresh browser session/login with two different agents (atsOptimization and notification) -> identical raw-JSON error both times.
expected: Test Run should either omit Planned/deterministic agents from its own selectable dropdown (mirroring the Run button's own runnable gating on the card), or the frontend schema should honestly accept the documented null shape and show a friendly message (e.g. 'This agent has no LLM cost to preview' for deterministic agents, 'Not yet available' for Planned ones) — never a raw Zod validation object.
observed: 18 of 22 dropdown entries (82%) produce a truncated, technical, machine-readable JSON error string rendered directly in the paying user's UI inside a role="alert" box, violating the 'no raw stack traces/5xx JSON in UI' requirement even though the underlying HTTP response is a clean 200 — the leak is purely a frontend contract bug, not a server error.

summary: The Anthropic Claude provider card permanently shows the green 'Connected · Manage' status/action (status:"connected") even after a live, real 'Test connection' round-trip to api.anthropic.com proves the stored credential is broken (HTTP 401, 'OAuth access token has expired'). The only visible sign of the real failure is a small, neutrally-styled detail line ('...last verify: failed') beneath the still-green badge — a paying user relying on the badge/button color would reasonably believe Anthropic is actively working.
reproduction: 1. Log in as admin/admin123 (Pro entitlement); navigate to https://5cb5f0620.abacusai.cloud/dashboard/agents.

2. Observe the Anthropic Claude provider card: green dot, badge 'Saved in app', action button 'Connected · Manage' (green), detail text 'Credential stored in the encrypted vault (…eAAA) · last verify: ok' (a stale prior success).

3. Click 'Connected · Manage' to open the config modal, then click 'Test connection' (data-testid=provider-config-verify) -- a genuine, non-simulated round-trip (POST /agents/providers/anthropic/verify) to api.anthropic.com.

4. Observe the honest failure notice: 'Anthropic Claude connection failed — anthropic returned HTTP 401: {"type":"error","error":{"type":"authentication_error","message":"OAuth access token has expired. Re-authenticate to continue."},"request_id":null}'.

5. Close the modal and re-read the main provider grid card: the detail line now correctly reads '...last verify: failed', BUT the badge is still 'Saved in app' (green) and the action button is still 'Connected · Manage' in the same green success styling used for a genuinely working provider -- confirmed via GET /agents/providers: {"id":"anthropic","status":"connected","lastVerifyStatus":"failed",...}. Root cause: apps/api/app/routers/agents.py:1713-1723 (_build_provider_entry) sets status='connected' unconditionally whenever a DB credential row exists (`if db: source='database'; status='connected'`), and the only downgrade path checks a separate, unrelated persisted admin `override` row (line 1746), never `last_verify_status`. The frontend (apps/web/src/components/agents/ProviderConnections.tsx:15-31, DOT/CARD_BORDER/ACTION_CLS) keys all styling strictly off `status`, so it has no way to reflect a failed verify short of the small unstyled detail sentence.

6. Reproduced in a SECOND, independent fresh browser session: the mismatch was observed persisted (no click) at load, then a second live Test-connection call was fired and returned the identical HTTP 401 failure, and the badge/action again remained green 'Connected · Manage' afterward.
7. Confirmed via a fresh page reload / browser back-navigation to the same route: the misleading green state survives a full reload (it is server-persisted, not a stale client cache).
expected: A provider whose most recent live verify failed should visually demote to the existing 'warning' state (yellow dot/badge, e.g. matching the wireframe's 'Re-authenticate' treatment already used for Google Gemini) rather than remain in the 'connected'/green state, so a paying user is not misled into thinking Anthropic-routed agent runs will succeed.
observed: status stays 'connected' (green, 'Connected · Manage') indefinitely regardless of lastVerifyStatus; only a small, neutral-colored detail sentence carries the true state, and the one-time toast notice produced by clicking Test Connection is transient and disappears on the next page load, leaving only the misleading green badge as the persistent, at-a-glance signal.
